content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission,
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814
Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement
(916) 322-5662          322-5660                322-5901            322-6441


May 31, 1984


Judy Fowler
319 Quail Terrace
Ben Lomond, CA  95005

                                        Re: Your Request for Advice
                                            Our No. A-84-140

Dear Ms. Fowler:

     This is to confirm advice rendered in response to your
telephone request for advice on May 25, 1984. You are a member
of the Board of Directors of the San Lorenzo Valley Water
District, a public agency. As such, you are a public official
subject to the conflict of interest provisions of the Political
Reform Act.1/  The District has designated various areas for
eventual connection to sewer facilities and these areas have
been ranked by priority, with "Class 1" being scheduled to
receive services first. Existing residences currently utilizing
septic tanks will be required to connect to the sewer facilities
when those services are extended to their areas. This will be
costly for these homeowners.

     Your grandparents reside in a home which is situated within
an area currently designated as "Class 1." The residents of the
street where they live (including your grandparents) have
requested that their street be removed from Class 1. This would
have the practical effect of saving your grandparents a
substantial sum for sewer connection costs. You have no
financial relationship with your grandparents however, in light
of their close familial relationship to you and in light of the
foregoing facts, you wish to know whether or not you are
required to disqualify yourself from participating in the
decision on whether to grant the request for removal from the
"Class 1" area.


1/  Government Code Sections 81000-91014. All statutory
references are to the Government Code.

Judy Fowler
May 31, 1984
Page 2


     As I advised you over the telephone, the familial
relationship with your grandparents is not a basis for
disqualification under the Political Reform Act.  The Act
prevents official actions where an appearance of conflict of
interest exists because of an official's private financial
dealings.  The Act currently does not deal with interpersonal
relationships.  Since you have no financial relationship with
your grandparents (you do not have an investment in them, are
not employed by them and they are not a source of income to
you), there is no basis for disqualification under the Political
Reform Act.

     Should you have any questions regarding this advice, please
do not hesitate to call me at (916) 322-5901.

                                   Sincerely,


                                   Robert E. Leidigh
                                   Counsel
                                   Legal Division

REL:plh

sections.question: You are a member of the Board of Directors of the San Lorenzo Valley Water District. Your grandparents reside in a home situated within an area designated as "Class 1" for sewer connection priority. The residents of your grandparents' street have requested removal from Class 1, which would save your grandparents substantial costs for sewer connection. You have no financial relationship with your grandparents. Are you required to disqualify yourself from participating in the decision on whether to grant the request for removal from the "Class 1" area?
sections.conclusion: The familial relationship with your grandparents is not a basis for disqualification under the Political Reform Act. The Act prevents official actions where an appearance of conflict of interest exists because of an official's private financial dealings. The Act currently does not deal with interpersonal relationships. Since you have no financial relationship with your grandparents (you do not have an investment in them, are not employed by them and they are not a source of income to you), there is no basis for disqualification under the Political Reform Act.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a public official must disqualify themselves from voting on a matter affecting their grandparents' property due to familial relationship, absent any financial interest.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: No disqualification is required under the Political Reform Act because the official has no financial relationship with their grandparents. The Act addresses only financial conflicts of interest, not familial relationships.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Clear FPPC advice letter with telephone request confirmation. Question and conclusion sections identified and extracted verbatim. Document references Government Code sections 81000-91014 regarding conflict of interest provisions.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: You are a member of the Board of Directors of the San Lorenzo Valley Water District. Your grandparents reside in a home situated within an area designated as "Class 1" for sewer connection priority. The residents of your grandparents' street have requested removal from Class 1, which would save your grandparents substantial costs for sewer connection. You have no financial relationship with your grandparents. Are you required to disqualify yourself from participating in the decision on whether to grant the request for removal from the "Class 1" area?

CONCLUSION: The familial relationship with your grandparents is not a basis for disqualification under the Political Reform Act. The Act prevents official actions where an appearance of conflict of interest exists because of an official's private financial dealings. The Act currently does not deal with interpersonal relationships. Since you have no financial relationship with your grandparents (you do not have an investment in them, are not employed by them and they are not a source of income to you), there is no basis for disqualification under the Political Reform Act.

embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter confirming telephone counsel to a San Lorenzo Valley Water District board member regarding whether familial relationship with grandparents requires disqualification from voting on sewer service priority decisions affecting their property.
